verses.text: 'Son of man, stand upon thy feet,' God says, and sends him to 'a rebellious nation.' He is raised to be commissioned. God lifts the fallen worshipper and gives him work to do.
'Son of man, stand upon thy feet,' God says, and sends him to 'a rebellious nation.' He is raised to be commissioned. God lifts the fallen worshipper and gives him work to do.
'Son of man, stand upon thy feet,' God says, and sends him to 'a rebellious nation.' He is raised to be commissioned. God lifts the fallen worshipper and gives him work to do.

They are 'impudent... and stiffhearted,' yet whether they hear or refuse, 'they shall know that there hath been a prophet among them.' Faithfulness, not success, is the charge. The messenger's duty is to speak, the result is God's.

They are 'impudent... and stiffhearted,' yet whether they hear or refuse, 'they shall know that there hath been a prophet among them.' Faithfulness, not success, is the charge. The messenger's duty is to speak, the result is God's.
'Be not afraid' of them, though they be briers and thorns and he dwell among scorpions; only speak God's words. Courage is commanded. The fear of hostile faces must not silence the truth.
'Be not afraid' of them, though they be briers and thorns and he dwell among scorpions; only speak God's words. Courage is commanded. The fear of hostile faces must not silence the truth.

He is told to eat what God gives; a hand unrolls a scroll 'written within and without' with 'lamentations, and mourning, and woe.' The message is solemn. The prophet must first take God's word inward before he speaks it out.
